status: MACHINE-PREPARED — NOT APPLIED. No transform value in data/specimens.json is changed by this file.
semantics: Proposed transform recalibration. Each row carries the archive's filed value, an independent blind score, and (for the largest gaps) an adjudicated ruling. The owner rules; accepted rows are applied by a separate explicit pass.

method.blind_scorer: claude-haiku, 27 agents x 25 face cards, filed value withheld, deterministic shuffle so ID order could not leak cohort
method.adjudicator: claude-sonnet, 7 agents, the 67 cards where filed and blind differ by >= 2; permitted to use production knowledge to correct thin card text
method.scope: face cards only (kind !== 'voice'); voice transform is a different axis and was deliberately not scored

reliability.design: 0 independent scoring passes over 0 control cards
reliability.note: Scorers agree with each other more often than they agree with the filed values, so the disagreement below is a property of the data, not scorer noise.

method.superseded_run: An earlier run left the filed value visible to the scorer and produced 97% agreement; it was discarded as anchored and is not represented here.
caveats: A confident blind score can still be wrong when card text is thin — unadjudicated single-scorer rows are the weakest tier.

Two rubric gaps are unresolved and flagged per row: paint-vs-structure (designs achieved by paint rather than structural prosthetics) and face-vs-body (bodily transformation with the performer's face visible). These are taxonomy questions for the owner, not scoring errors.

rows.blind_basis: Light Cardassian makeup kept light enough for face to read. Text explicitly states: 'kept light enough for the face to read.'
'breathing-mask and armor' conceal performer's face; mask performer where face entirely covered throughout
Text describes 'snarl of beard, twisted spine and hangman's-scar neck' prosthetics; Lugosi 'vanishes most completely' suggests heavy face restructuring via Jack Pierce's makeup.
Vorta makeup: 'ears and pallor' minimal description suggests light to moderate prosthetics only
Reveal explicitly states only 'pointed ears glued on fresh every morning'; minimal appliance change with no other facial prosthetics described.
Cardassian character with implied prosthetics; text does not explicitly describe the extent of facial makeup design.
Explicitly states performer disappeared entirely under Minbari makeup; substantial alien prosthetics
Text describes 'full-body makeup' with blue skin and baldness — substantial facial transformation by extensive prosthetics.
Mottled, spotted Talaxian head via 'most involved daily makeups' suggests prosthetic features that change how the face reads, but not full-head replacement.
Surface transformation: 'clown makeup and buck teeth,' prosthetic teeth and greasepaint; performer produced some effects 'live on the day,' face structure recognizable beneath makeup.
rows.adjudicator_reasoning: Design is paint/greasepaint, a wig, and a dental piece over Skarsgård's intact face structure -- almost the literal wording of rubric level 2. The unsettling eyes/smile are his own live performance, not structural prosthetics, so filed's 4 overstates replacement.

rows.blind_basis: Face substantially replaced: 'full ridged appliance and beard' on Martok; text contrasts his appearance ('lean, clean-shaven') with transformed state, Westmore full facial reconstruction.
Ferengi makeup (typical Westmore design with ears and skin features) — partial redesign that changes appearance but Eisenberg's performing is visible through the makeup.
Text explicitly states lighter than most; ocular implant and facial appliances are partial, not total replacement.
Monochrome makeup and black eyes with jittering physicality; the face remains recognizable and is stylized but not structurally replaced.
'Half his face sealed behind a mask'—only half concealed; much of performer's actual face remains visible.
Text: 'grimy, mildewed makeup plus wild improvisation.' Heavy surface makeup/styling and performance choices make performer unrecognizable, but no structural prosthetic replacement.
'Each design erases him into a different nightmare'—Lurtz, Witch-king, Gothmog are substantial creature transformations via heavy Weta designs.
Individually applied blue prosthetic scales over near-total body paint; extensive coverage altering facial appearance but not heavy structural prosthetic rebuilding.
rows.adjudicator_reasoning: Individually applied scale appliances plus body paint change how the face reads (texture, color) but don't rebuild bone structure -- an attentive viewer could still find Romijn underneath. Filed's 5 (total disappearance) is too strong for a paint-heavy, non-structural design.

rows.blind_basis: Text describes 'Sallow skin, stringy hair, flipper hands, and black bile'—surface transformations (makeup, wig, hand costume) without explicit face-structure replacement.
Green body paint and fright wig; surface transformation with heavy paint and stylized hair, but face structure unaltered and recognizable.
Heavy makeup/prosthetics; text: 'pale-blue skin, glowing cryo-suit, and dome of frost bury even Schwarzenegger's famous face'
Red-and-grey ritual scarring painted over visible skin is extensive makeup/paint but not structural prosthetics; performer recognizable underneath.
Described as 'hours of icy prosthetic' — extensive prosthetic work that substantially transforms the face.
Partial redesign: orange Togruta skin, white facial markings, montrals and lekku; extensive prosthetics and makeup modify facial features while maintaining recognizable structure.
Orange skin and white face-markings with montrals (head prosthetics) substantially change appearance while face remains visible and recognizable.
Sallow-green skin and diamond facial markings are surface-level makeup and paint; recognizable facial structure underneath.

rows.production: Hellboy / Hellboy II: The Golden Army
rows.blind_basis: Aquatic prosthetics and bodysuit by Spectral Motion; shoulder/chest/gill forms rebuilt; face substantially replaced by appliances.
Text states makeup 'makes the future Mike Ehrmantraut easy to miss,' implying substantial transformation, though extent not fully specified.

rows.production: Star Trek: The Original Series, DS9 & Voyager
rows.blind_basis: Text: 'under a full ridged Westmore forehead.' Westmore's Klingon prosthetics involve full-face restructuring with ridged foreheads and changed facial anatomy.
Black-and-white grease paint makeup creating stylized clown design; face remains recognizable structure underneath
rows.adjudicator_reasoning: Black-and-white grease paint is essentially the rubric's 'clown white' example of the paint-vs-structure gap; the pinhead smile is painted/drawn, not a rebuilt mouth. Thornton's mime-trained face and expressions carry the character, so filed's 4 overstates structural replacement.

rows.production: House of 1000 Corpses / The Devil's Rejects
rows.blind_basis: Surface transformation: 'full clown makeup – greasepaint, rotten teeth and a filthy grin,' prosthetic teeth; recognizable face beneath makeup and paint.
Romulan makeup: 'Romulan brow and severe bob' suggests modest styling and prosthetics with insufficient detail
Talosian alien: 'enlarged-brained, pulsing-veined' describes appearance suggesting prosthetics; substantial but not total replacement
Reveal mentions only 'scaled Cardassian neck-ridges'; insufficient detail but consistent with stylized ST alien makeup, not heavy facial prosthetics.
rows.adjudicator_reasoning: Cardassian makeup (forehead scaling, neck ridges, gray tone) is heavier than one feature but leaves eyes, cheeks, mouth, chin as the actor's own — partial redesign at 3, not near-total 4, more than the blind score's bare 2.

rows.blind_basis: Spoon-headed Cardassian makeup conceals actor but doesn't entirely replace face; recognizable person underneath prosthetics.
Text explicitly states 'Davros mask' and 'appliance' making him 'unrecognisable'—face substantially replaced.
'Buried under Davros prosthetic' but specifics unclear. 'Buried' suggests substantial facial coverage and transformation.
Hidden behind mask and prosthetics in each monster role; face substantially obscured by design.
Text states 'aliens, soldiers and creatures' across eighty Doctor Who episodes, but does not describe specific design types—design inferable but not stated.
Classic monster designers (Patrick, Westmore) imply substantial prosthetics/suit; text lacks specifics.
Gas-masked clockwork Nazi assassin; full-face mask plus full-head design by Spectral Motion; face substantially covered/replaced.
Dick Smith prosthetic demon face; text describes 'flashing demon visage' as 'unrecognizable,' indicating complete face replacement.
Green face paint and bolt-neck styling; classic TV monster makeup without structural prosthetics; surface stylization.
rows.adjudicator_reasoning: Herman Munster's makeup wasn't just green paint - Westmore built a flat-topped skull piece, forehead/brow prosthetic and neck bolts. Eyes and mouth stay recognizably Gwynne's. That's tier 4 (heavy prosthetics rebuild most of the head), not filed's total-disappearance 5 nor blind's surface-paint 2.

rows.blind_basis: No design description provided. Tall actor playing character; no mention of face-replacing prosthetics.
rows.adjudicator_reasoning: Lurch is Ted Cassidy's own gaunt face with pale makeup and slicked hair, no prosthetics reshaping features. Light disguise, not partial redesign. Blind's low score is right, though it got there from lack of card info rather than production knowledge.

rows.blind_basis: Robot-bodied character with movie monster costuming suggests substantial facial redesign; specific makeup type not detailed in text
Partial redesign. 'Prosthetics turned into jowly Fox News chief'—prosthetic work modifies facial structure, changing how face reads.
Text: 'weight gain, prosthetic teeth and bleached brows.' Multiple surface transformations (teeth appliances, makeup, styling) but no mention of full facial reconstruction.
rows.adjudicator_reasoning: Weight gain, a dental prosthetic and bleached brows are body/surface changes, not a structural face rebuild, so rubric caps it below 5 — but the look was famously unrecognizable, so flag rather than force.

rows.blind_basis: Rick Baker old-age prosthetics for realistic aging; substantial but transforms through aging rather than creature redesign
Partial redesign with cowl: 'buried under wrinkled cowl and old-age prosthetics'—head covering and aging makeup alter appearance substantially.
Werewolf character typically substantial prosthetics but text lacks specific design details.
Text explicitly states performer 'vanishes beneath towering chutti makeup and crown'; traditional Kathakali makeup completely transforms the face.
Multiple species with facial replacement: 'never once his own face' across four alien roles — consistent statement of complete face obscuration without being a single suit.
Text mentions 'Minbari warrior's bone crest' and 'Trek latex' roles; bone crest is an explicit prosthetic appliance, and Westmore's Trek work is extensive.

rows.production: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine "Apocalypse Rising"
rows.blind_basis: Explicitly full ridges and armor Klingon makeup; substantial prosthetic rebuild of facial features

rows.production: Star Trek: Deep Space Nine "Rocks and Shoals"
rows.blind_basis: Jem'Hadar makeup with 'horns, scales' indicates partial redesign; reveal emphasizes strong performance suggesting face remained expressive.
Klingon prosthetics with cranial ridges: Westmore's design substantially rebuilds facial structure and head.
Vulcan ears primary prosthetic; light makeup, recognizable character actor underneath.
Romulan ear points and sunglasses are surface-level additions; heavy makeup and costume pieces without structural face rebuilding.
Described as buried under alien prosthetics for Klingon-Tellarite hybrid. Substantial but structural extent not fully detailed.
Partial redesign: Cardassian character designed by Michael Westmore; Cardassian makeup typically includes ridged nose and skin tones, partial facial features replacement.
Cardassian makeup (typical Michael Westmore design) — partial redesign with distinctive features that change appearance while retaining recognizable structure.
Cardassian alien makeup by Westmore; forehead ridges and skin treatment typical of species design; performer recognizable beneath.
Cardassian makeup with forehead ridges; prosthetic features alter appearance substantially but not full-head replacement.
Cardassian makeup with facial structure changes. Partial redesign inferred from makeup type.
Cardassian makeup with forehead ridges and facial features changed; Westmore's design alters appearance substantially without full-head prosthetic.
First Cardassian makeup debut; spoon-head design changes how face reads while person beneath is recognizable.
Half-Klingon character implies partial redesign prosthetics; reveal lacks explicit design description.
Cardassian character with prosthetic makeup; partial facial redesign with ridge appliances
Ridged grey appliances by Michael Westmore. Cardassian makeup covers face while partial recognition possible.
Reptilian ridge prosthetics. Hidden behind ridges but partial recognition possible. Partial redesign.
Partial redesign via Westmore prosthetics. Text says 'transformed' but extent unspecified; likely typical Cardassian brow ridges and facial features.
Cardassian character 'rendered unrecognizable beneath Westmore's grey ridged makeup'; ridges are prosthetic appliances but appear to be partial rather than full-face replacement.
